sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content:
The list view is currently a flat, read-only table — useful for scanning node names but not for *working* with the index. Developers cannot group nodes by meaningful categories (type, module, staleness), cannot see code without leaving the viz, cannot verify nodes without the CLI, and have no way to spawn a focused graph from their current filtered set.

The sidebar is shared with the graph view and lacks list-specific controls like group-by selectors, saved filter presets, and batch action buttons. As a result, the list view is a passive display rather than an active workbench — developers default to the CLI for anything beyond browsing.

The list view should become the primary workspace: filter, group, inspect code, verify, and then spawn a graph for the subset that needs visual exploration.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-LV-01 — Group nodes by category**
As a developer, I want to group list rows by type, subtype, module/file, staleness, or tag so that I can see related nodes together and collapse groups I don't care about — instead of scrolling a flat alphabetical list.

**US-LV-02 — Focus on a single function's code**
As a developer, I want a "View Source" action that opens a code panel focused on the selected function's line range — isolating it from surrounding code — so that I can review the exact code a node represents without noise.

**US-LV-03 — Dedicated list sidebar**
As a developer, I want a list-specific sidebar with group-by selector, multi-select staleness filter (checkboxes, not radio), and action buttons (Run Check, Verify Selected, View in Graph) so that I have a proper control surface for list-based workflows.

**US-LV-04 — Verify nodes from the list**
As a developer, I want to click a verify icon on a list row or select multiple rows and bulk-verify with a shared reason so that I can triage staleness directly in the viz without switching to the CLI.

**US-LV-05 — Run check and see results live**
As a developer, I want a "Run Check" button in the list toolbar that triggers staleness recomputation and updates the list in place — highlighting nodes whose status changed and re-grouping by staleness — so that I can triage without leaving the list.

**US-LV-06 — Spawn a graph from filtered nodes**
As a developer, I want to select nodes in the list (via checkboxes or current filter) and click "View in Graph" to open a graph scoped to those nodes plus their connecting edges so that the graph shows me exactly what I'm investigating — not every node in the project.

**US-LV-07 — Understand why a node is stale**
As a developer, I want a clickable staleness badge on each row that shows a tooltip/popover explaining the cause (which hash changed, which linked node triggered it, which child caused inheritance) so that I know what action to take without opening the detail panel.

**US-LV-08 — See source location as clickable**
As a developer, I want the Location column to be a clickable link that opens the file in my editor (via VS Code URI or similar) so that I can jump to the code directly.

**US-LV-09 — Column customization**
As a developer, I want to show/hide columns (name, type, subtype, location, staleness, tags, last verified, last modified) so that I can tailor the table to my current task.

**US-LV-10 — Filter by time range ("since")**
As a developer reviewing a PR or recent work, I want a "Since" filter in the toolbar that narrows the list to nodes with history events after a reference point (last checkpoint, a git SHA, or a date) so that I can see exactly what changed in a specific unit of work — and then use group-by, graph spawn, and diff as normal on that filtered set.

**US-LV-11 — See change summary per node in filtered view**
As a developer using the "since" filter, I want a "Changes" column showing a compact summary of what happened to each node in the filtered window (e.g. "CODE_ONLY ×2", "AGENT_VERIFIED", "INITIAL") so that I can scan the list and understand the nature of each change without opening the detail panel.

**US-LV-12 — Generate impact report from current view**
As a developer, I want a "Generate Report" button in the toolbar (visible when a "since" filter is active) that produces a structured markdown impact report summarizing the current filtered view — unverified nodes, agent-verified nodes, new nodes, test results — so that I can attach it to a PR or share it as a review artifact.

**US-LV-13 — Diff view for stale nodes**
As a developer viewing a CONTENT_UPDATED node in the list, I want a "Diff" toggle in the source panel that shows a side-by-side comparison (Monaco diff editor) of the code at last verification vs current, so I can judge whether the staleness is cosmetic or substantive without leaving the viz.

**US-LV-14 — Graceful diff fallback**
As a developer clicking Diff on a node that was never verified or whose baseline SHA is unavailable (GC'd after rebase, new node), I want to see the Diff button disabled with a tooltip ("No baseline available — node has not been verified yet") so I understand this is expected and don't think something is broken.

**US-LV-15 — Diff from history timeline**
As a developer viewing a node's history tab in the detail panel, I want to click any SHA-bearing history entry to see the diff at that specific point in the source panel, so I can trace how the code evolved over time.

**US-LV-16 — Doc diff in source panel**
As a developer viewing a doc node with the since filter active, I want a Diff toggle in the doc preview panel that shows word-level insertions (green) and deletions (red) per section, with unchanged sections collapsed. When viewing a section node, the diff scopes to just that section; the Full Doc toggle expands to all sections while staying in diff mode. The diff compares the DocJSON content at the baseline submodule commit against the current version, using jsdiff `Diff.diffWords()` for fine-grained highlighting. New sections show as fully green, removed sections as fully red with a badge.

**US-LV-17 — Ghost nodes for deleted entries**
As a developer using the since filter, I want deleted nodes to appear as dimmed strikethrough rows with a red "deleted" badge in the staleness column, so I'm aware of what was removed since the baseline. Ghost nodes have no checkbox, no click handler, no detail button, and no VS Code link — they are informational only. The since endpoint returns ghost node metadata synthesized from preserved DELETED history rows for node_ids that no longer exist in the nodes table.

**US-LV-18 — Hierarchical focus traversal for doc sections**
As a developer viewing a nested doc section in the source panel, I want Up/Down buttons that navigate the section hierarchy — Up zooms out to the parent section or full document, Down zooms into the first child section — so I can traverse the document structure without lateral movement, always relative to the currently selected node.

**Linked nodes:**
- `cortex::cortex.diff::get_doc_diff` — get_doc_diff(db_path, project_root, doc_id, baseline_sha=None) — Return old vs new sections for a doc, diffing across submodule commits.  @ cortex/diff.py#L239-L388
- `cortex::cortex.viz.server::get_doc_diff_endpoint` — get_doc_diff_endpoint(doc_id, sha=None) — Return section-level diff of a DocJSON file against a baseline commit.  @ cortex/viz/server.py#L2292-L2309
- `cortex::cortex.viz.server::get_history_since_endpoint` — get_history_since_endpoint(sha=None, timestamp=None) — Return node IDs that changed since a reference point.  @ cortex/viz/server.py#L695-L789

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| Sortable HTML table (Title, Type, Location, Staleness, Tags) | Done |
| Click-to-sort column headers with direction indicators | Done |
| Row click opens detail panel with node info | Done |
| Staleness badges with color-coded CSS classes | Done |
| Type badges with subtype-based coloring | Done |
| Tag chips (first 5 per row) | Done |
| Node count in toolbar | Done |
| Shared sidebar filters (type, subtype, tags, status, staleness) | Done |
| Search integration (debounced full-text) | Done |
| Large project default (>400 nodes → list view) | Done |
| Source endpoint `GET /api/nodes/{id}/source` for code preview | Done |
| Row grouping by category (type, subtype, module/file, staleness, tag) | Done |
| Collapsible group headers with counts | Done |
| Nested directory tree grouping for location mode (indented by depth) | Done |
| Collapse All / Expand All toggle in toolbar | Done |
| Dedicated list sidebar with group-by selector and action buttons | Done |
| Verify button per row (checkmark icon, hover-visible on stale rows) | Done |
| Bulk verify via checkbox selection + toolbar action | Done |
| "Run Check" button with live result update | Done |
| Staleness cause tooltip on badge click (server-side data, cached) | Done |
| Clickable location column (VS Code URI with absolute path) | Done |
| Inline "source" / "doc" link in Name column (always visible) | Done |
| Right-side source column with Monaco (replaces bottom split) | Done |
| DocJSON nodes render as formatted HTML in source column (not raw JSON) | Done |
| Focus Function toggle (full file vs isolated function view) | Done |
| Column show/hide customization (6-column menu) | Done |
| Spawn graph from selected/filtered nodes (client-side edge filter) | Done |
| Hide private (`_`-prefixed) functions by default + toggle | Done |
| Test path filter using `test_paths` from cortex.toml + toggle | Done |
| Tags filter redesign (docs-style panel: chips, search, count sort) | Done |
| "Since" time filter (checkpoint / SHA / date) | Done (Phase 7 — US-LV-10) |
| "Changes" column (compact change summary per node when since filter active) | Not started |
| "Generate Report" button (impact report from filtered view) | Not started |
| Diff toggle in source panel (Monaco diff editor, baseline vs current) | Done (Phase 6 — US-LV-13) |
| Ghost nodes for deleted entries in since filter view | Done (Phase 8 — dimmed strikethrough rows) |
| Doc diff view (word-level jsdiff for DocJSON nodes) | Done (Phase 8) |
| Saved filter presets (`.cortex/saved_views.json`) | Not started |
| Hierarchical Up/Down traversal for nested docjson nodes | Not started |

sections.heading: Sidebar Specification
sections.content:
The list view gets its own sidebar replacing the shared graph/list sidebar when list view is active. Structure:

```
[Search box]
─── Since ───
  ○ All time (default)
  ○ Since last checkpoint
  ○ Since commit… (SHA input)
  ○ Since date… (date picker)
  [Generate Report]  (visible when since ≠ "All time")
─── Group By ───
  ○ None (flat)
  ○ By Type
  ○ By Module/File
  ○ By Staleness
  ○ By Tag
─── Filters ───
  ▸ Node Type (checkboxes)
  ▸ Subtype (checkboxes)
  ▸ Staleness (checkboxes, multi-select)
  ▸ Tags (pill multi-select, AND logic)
  ▸ Status
─── Actions ───
  [Run Check]
  [Verify Selected (N)]
  [View in Graph →]
─── Saved Views ───
  • "Stale code" (built-in preset)
  • "Needs attention" (built-in preset)
  • "Undocumented" (built-in preset)
  [+ Save current view]
```

Key differences from graph sidebar:
- **Since filter** — time-scopes the node list to a reference point. When active, a "Changes" column appears in the table showing per-node change summaries, and a "Generate Report" button becomes visible
- **Multi-select staleness** (checkboxes) instead of radio buttons — the list can show multiple staleness states simultaneously since grouping handles visual separation
- **Group-by selector** — not applicable to graph view
- **Action buttons** — verify and spawn-graph are list-specific workflows
- **Saved views** — persisted to `.cortex/saved_views.json`, per-project

The "since" filter composes with all other filters. "Since last checkpoint + staleness = CONTENT_UPDATED" shows exactly the nodes that went stale in the current work window and haven't been addressed — the core PR review workflow.

sections.heading: Code Preview Specification
sections.content:
The code preview feature has three tiers:

**Tier 1 — Inline preview (list column)**
A new "Code" column shows the first 3 lines of the node's source code in a monospace font with syntax highlighting (lightweight — CSS-only keyword coloring, not full Monaco). Collapsed by default. Click to expand to ~10 lines.

**Tier 2 — Full source panel (bottom split)**
Clicking "View Source" on a row (or pressing a hotkey) opens a bottom panel with the full file loaded in Monaco (already available via CDN for docs view). The node's line range is highlighted and scrolled into view.

**Tier 3 — Focus function toggle**
A toggle button in the source panel switches between "Full File" and "Focus Function" mode. In Focus mode, Monaco shows only the lines in the node's `location` range (e.g., lines 42-78), hiding everything else. This isolates the exact code the node represents.

**Backend requirement:**
New `GET /api/nodes/{id}/source` endpoint that reads the file at the node's `level_3_location`, extracts the line range from `location`, and returns `{file_path, full_source, start_line, end_line, focused_source}`. Respects `.cortex/cortex.toml` skip patterns for security.

sections.heading: Backend Changes Required
sections.content:
| Endpoint | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `GET /api/nodes/{id}/source` | Return source code for a node (full file + focused line range) |
| `POST /api/nodes/bulk-verify` | Batch verification for selected nodes |
| `GET /api/nodes/{id}/staleness-cause` | Return staleness cause via server-side joins (edges, verification, children) — no new DB column needed |
| `GET /api/nodes/{id}/diff` | Return old vs current source for a node (baseline from last verification SHA via `git show`) |
| `GET /api/impact` | Return nodes with history events since a reference point (checkpoint/SHA/date), enriched with per-node change summaries, verification status, and test results. Serves both the "since" filter in viz and the `cortex report` CLI command |
| `GET /api/saved-views` | List saved filter presets from `.cortex/saved_views.json` |
| `POST /api/saved-views` | Create/update a saved filter preset |
| `DELETE /api/saved-views/{name}` | Delete a saved filter preset |

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
**Row grouping engine (US-LV-01)** — Implement a client-side grouping engine in `list.js` that buckets `App.state.filteredNodes` by a selected field (node_type, subtype, staleness, location file prefix, first tag). Render collapsible `<tbody>` sections with group headers showing name and count. Persist last-used grouping in session storage.

**Code preview column and source panel (US-LV-02, US-LV-03)** — Add the `GET /api/nodes/{id}/source` endpoint. On the frontend, add an optional "Code" column with 3-line preview (CSS keyword highlighting). Clicking "View Source" opens a bottom split panel using Monaco. The "Focus Function" toggle filters Monaco to the node's line range only.

**Dedicated list sidebar (US-LV-04)** — Fork the current sidebar into `sidebar-graph.js` and `sidebar-list.js`. The list sidebar uses checkboxes for staleness (multi-select), adds group-by radio selector, action buttons (Run Check, Verify Selected, View in Graph), and a saved views section. `App.switchView()` swaps the sidebar when toggling between list and graph.

**Verify from list (US-LV-05)** — Add a checkmark icon to each row (visible on hover for clean rows, always visible for stale rows). Clicking it calls `POST /api/nodes/{id}/verify` with `verified_by: 'human'`. For bulk: checkbox column + toolbar button calls `POST /api/nodes/bulk-verify`. Both show a reason popover before submitting.

**Spawn graph from list (US-LV-07)** — "View in Graph" button collects the current filtered node IDs (or checkbox-selected IDs), switches to graph view, and calls `Graph.loadAll()` with only those nodes and their connecting edges (fetched via a new `POST /api/subgraph` endpoint accepting `{node_ids: [...]}`). Graph breadcrumb shows "Showing N nodes from list filter: ...".

**Saved views (US-LV-09)** — Store presets in `.cortex/saved_views.json` as `{name, filters: {types, subtypes, staleness, tags, status}, group_by, columns}`. Three built-in presets: "Stale code" (staleness != VERIFIED, group by staleness), "Needs attention" (staleness = CONTENT_UPDATED/NOT_FOUND), "Undocumented" (no inbound documents edge, type = function). Users can save custom presets from the sidebar.

**Virtual scrolling** — If projects exceed 2000 nodes after filtering, implement virtual scrolling (render only visible rows + buffer) to maintain smooth scrolling. Lower priority since grouping with collapsed sections reduces visible row count.
